patterns.title: Bottom microphone thermal sensor failure (mic1)
patterns.diagnosis: The mic1 NTC thermal sensor on the lightning/charging flex cable is disconnected or damaged.

probabilities.description: The NTC sensor is soldered on the flex. Flex replacement resolves.
Codec IC (338S00509) not reading sensor. Requires micro-soldering.
Standalone NTC failure. Replaceable with micro-soldering.

repair_steps: 1. Replace lightning/charging flex cable assembly
2. Test bottom microphone in Voice Memos (record + playback)
3. Verify thermal sensor reads in diagnostics
4. If persists: inspect codec IC under microscope
test_procedure: Voice Memos: record 10s, play back — should hear clear audio from bottom mic
Phone call: speaker on mute, other party should hear you clearly
Diagnostics: Settings > Privacy > Analytics > check for new panic-full logs

patterns.title: Side/rear microphone thermal sensor failure (mic2)
patterns.diagnosis: The mic2 NTC thermal sensor (near volume buttons or rear mic) is disconnected.

probabilities.description: mic2 sensor on the rear camera or side flex. Replace flex.
Connector dislodged during previous repair.
Trace to mic2 sensor broken. Requires micro-soldering.

repair_steps: 1. Reseat or replace rear camera/mic flex cable
2. Check FPC connector on logic board under microscope
3. Test all microphones (Voice Memos, speakerphone, Siri)
4. If persists: board-level trace repair
test_procedure: Voice Memos: record with phone facing away (rear mic test)
Video recording: check audio clarity
Monitor Analytics for new panic-full logs after repair

patterns.title: Top microphone thermal sensor failure (mic3)
patterns.diagnosis: The mic3 NTC thermal sensor near the earpiece/top speaker is disconnected.

probabilities.description: mic3 is on the earpiece/front sensor flex. Replace flex.
Flex damaged during screen replacement. Reseat or replace.
FPC connector damaged. Micro-soldering required.

repair_steps: 1. Replace earpiece/proximity sensor flex cable
2. Verify all front sensors reconnected properly
3. Test top microphone (Siri, phone calls)
4. If persists: inspect FPC connector on board
test_procedure: Siri activation and voice recognition test
Phone call quality (earpiece mic)
Check for new panic logs after 24h

patterns.title: Ambient light sensor failure (ALS)
patterns.diagnosis: The ambient light sensor on the front flex cable is disconnected or damaged. Auto-brightness will not work.

probabilities.description: ALS is on the proximity/earpiece flex. Replace flex.
Flex not transferred or damaged during screen swap.
ALS controller IC failed. Micro-soldering required.

repair_steps: 1. Replace proximity/earpiece sensor flex cable
2. If aftermarket screen: ensure ALS flex is transferred from original
3. Test auto-brightness in Settings > Display
4. If persists: ALS IC on board (micro-soldering)
test_procedure: Auto-brightness: cover sensor, screen should dim
Settings > Accessibility > Display > Auto-Brightness toggle
Monitor for new panic-full logs

patterns.title: Proximity sensor failure
patterns.diagnosis: The proximity sensor is disconnected. Screen stays on during calls (burns ear, pocket dials).

probabilities.description: Proximity sensor on the earpiece/front flex. Replace flex.
Aftermarket screen without proximity flex. Transfer from OEM.
FPC connector pins bent or corroded.

repair_steps: 1. Replace or reseat proximity/earpiece flex cable
2. For aftermarket screens: transfer OEM proximity flex
3. Test proximity during phone call (screen should turn off near ear)
4. If persists: inspect board FPC connector
test_procedure: Phone call: hold phone to ear, screen should turn off
Cover proximity sensor with finger — screen should blank
Check Settings > Phone > Proximity Sensor behavior

patterns.title: Barometer/pressure sensor failure (PRS0)
patterns.diagnosis: The PRS0 barometric pressure sensor is disconnected. Located on the charging port flex cable.

probabilities.description: PRS0 is on the lightning flex. Replace the entire flex.
Barometer IC failed. Usually resolves with flex replacement.
Trace to barometer broken on board.

repair_steps: 1. Replace lightning/charging port flex cable assembly
2. Test barometer: Compass app > elevation reading
3. Monitor for panic recurrence over 24h
test_procedure: Compass app: check if elevation/altitude is displayed
Weather app: verify local pressure reading
Health app: check flights climbed tracking

patterns.title: Battery temperature sensor failure (TG0B)
patterns.diagnosis: The battery NTC temperature sensor is disconnected or damaged. Device may refuse to charge.

probabilities.description: Battery flex not properly connected. Reseat connector.
NTC on battery flex is damaged. Replace battery.
Battery FPC connector pins damaged.

repair_steps: 1. Disconnect and reseat battery connector firmly
2. If persists: replace battery
3. Inspect board battery connector under microscope
4. If connector damaged: micro-soldering required
test_procedure: Check battery health: Settings > Battery > Battery Health
Verify charging works (both wired and wireless if supported)
Monitor temperature during use (should not overheat)

patterns.title: Battery voltage/fuel gauge sensor failure (TG0V)
patterns.diagnosis: The battery fuel gauge IC (bq27541) cannot communicate. Battery percentage may be inaccurate.

probabilities.description: Battery flex not seated. Reseat connector.
bq27541 chip on battery failed. Replace battery.
Communication line to fuel gauge broken on board.

repair_steps: 1. Disconnect and reseat battery connector
2. Replace battery if reseating doesn't help
3. Check gas gauge communication lines on board (advanced)
test_procedure: Check battery percentage accuracy (charge to 100%, use, verify drain is linear)
coconutBattery or 3uTools battery health check
Verify no unexpected shutdowns at >20% battery

patterns.title: Thermal monitoring service failure
patterns.diagnosis: thermalmonitord crashed or hung, usually due to a missing/disconnected thermal sensor.

probabilities.description: A sensor cable is damaged or not seated. Check panic string for 'Missing sensor' to identify which.
PMU or thermal controller IC fault.
Rare. Check if specific to one iOS version.

repair_steps: 1. Identify which sensor is missing from panic string
2. Map sensor to physical component (mic1=lightning flex, als=proximity flex, etc.)
3. Replace the corresponding flex cable
4. If no sensor identified: check all flex connections, reseat
test_procedure: Monitor for recurrence over 24h after repair
Check Analytics for new crash logs

patterns.title: GPU firmware lockup / hang
patterns.diagnosis: The GPU core locked up, triggering a firmware reset or kernel panic.

probabilities.description: Cold solder joint under SoC. Common after drop or thermal cycling.
GPU thermal paste dried, heatsink not seated.
Check if specific to one iOS version. Try DFU restore.

repair_steps: 1. DFU restore to rule out software
2. If persists: inspect board for signs of heat damage near SoC
3. Reball SoC (advanced micro-soldering, low success rate)
4. Board swap may be necessary
test_procedure: Run a graphics-intensive app (game or benchmark)
Monitor for GPU recovery messages in console
Check for visual artifacts on screen

patterns.title: Kernel data abort — invalid memory access
patterns.diagnosis: The kernel accessed an invalid memory address, causing an immediate panic.

probabilities.description: LPDDR4/5 cell failure. Not repairable.
Flash storage read error propagated to kernel. Try DFU restore first.
Rare. Check if tied to specific iOS version.

repair_steps: 1. DFU restore (rule out NAND filesystem corruption)
2. If persists: board-level diagnosis required
3. RAM is part of SoC package — not field-repairable
4. Board swap
test_procedure: After DFU restore, use phone normally for 48h
Run memory-intensive apps
Check Analytics for recurrence

patterns.title: Display/touch service timeout (backboardd)
patterns.diagnosis: backboardd (display + touch input service) stopped responding.

probabilities.description: Damaged or poorly seated display flex. Check FPC connector.
Connector pins bent or corroded on logic board.
Touch controller IC failure. Requires micro-soldering.

repair_steps: 1. Reseat display flex cable
2. Inspect FPC connector under microscope (bent/missing pins)
3. Try known-good display
4. If persists with good display: Meson IC or board issue
test_procedure: Touch responsiveness test (draw app, all screen areas)
3D Touch / Haptic Touch pressure test
Display color/dead pixel check

patterns.title: WiFi/Bluetooth service failure (wifid)
patterns.diagnosis: wifid stopped responding, indicating WiFi/BT chip communication failure.

probabilities.description: USI 339S00648 or similar. Common after water damage.
WiFi antenna cable not seated or damaged.
Try network settings reset, then DFU restore.

repair_steps: 1. Reset network settings
2. DFU restore
3. Check antenna connections
4. If persists: WiFi IC replacement (micro-soldering)
test_procedure: WiFi: connect to 2.4GHz and 5GHz networks, speed test
Bluetooth: pair AirPods or speaker
AirDrop: test file transfer
Check Settings > General > About > WiFi Address (if 'N/A' = chip dead)

patterns.title: Media services failure (mediaserverd)
patterns.diagnosis: mediaserverd hung — handles audio/video playback. Often related to audio IC or codec.

probabilities.description: Audio codec IC unresponsive. Common on iPhone 7/7+ (audio IC disease).
Audio output flex not seated. Reseat connections.
Try DFU restore first.

repair_steps: 1. DFU restore
2. Reseat speaker and earpiece flex cables
3. Test with known-good speaker assembly
4. If persists: audio IC replacement (micro-soldering)
test_procedure: Play music through speaker
Phone call: test earpiece audio
Voice Memos: record and playback
Check if Voice Memos app opens without freezing

patterns.title: Location service failure (locationd)
patterns.diagnosis: locationd hung — handles GPS/location. May indicate GPS antenna or baseband issue.

probabilities.description: GPS antenna cable not seated. Check connections.
GPS is handled by baseband IC. If no signal either, baseband fault.
Reset location services, try DFU restore.

repair_steps: 1. Reset Location & Privacy settings
2. DFU restore
3. Check GPS antenna cable connections
4. If persists with no cellular signal: baseband IC issue
test_procedure: Open Maps app — does location pin appear?
Compass app — does heading work?
Check cellular signal simultaneously

patterns.title: SpringBoard (home screen) failure
patterns.diagnosis: SpringBoard stopped responding. Usually software-related but can indicate memory issues.

probabilities.description: Filesystem or app cache corruption. DFU restore usually fixes.
Device nearly full, SpringBoard can't cache. Free up space.
If frequent after DFU restore, may indicate RAM failure.

repair_steps: 1. Force restart (volume up, volume down, hold power)
2. Free storage space if nearly full
3. DFU restore
4. If persists after restore: suspect RAM (board swap)
test_procedure: Normal usage for 24h after restore
Open 10+ apps rapidly, switch between them
Check storage: Settings > General > iPhone Storage

patterns.title: Secure Enclave Processor panic
patterns.diagnosis: The SEP crashed. This is a critical, board-level issue.

probabilities.description: SEP is part of the SoC. Not field-repairable.
Power rail issue affecting SEP.

repair_steps: 1. DFU restore (very unlikely to help but worth trying)
2. Board swap — SEP is fused to SoC, cannot be replaced independently
3. Device may be unrepairable if paired components are involved
test_procedure: Face ID / Touch ID test
Apple Pay enrollment test
Keychain access test

patterns.title: Memory pressure — app killed by Jetsam
patterns.diagnosis: iOS killed a process due to memory pressure. Usually normal behavior under heavy multitasking.

probabilities.description: Heavy app usage, many background processes. Not a hardware issue.
A specific app consuming excessive memory.
If Jetsam events are extremely frequent (100+/day), may indicate bad RAM cells.

repair_steps: 1. Check frequency — occasional Jetsam is normal
2. If excessive: identify the largest process in the log
3. Force-close heavy apps, restart device
4. If 100+ events/day: DFU restore, then suspect RAM if persists
test_procedure: Monitor Jetsam frequency over 24h
Note which apps trigger the most events
Compare to baseline (5-20/day is normal under active use)

patterns.title: Baseband/cellular modem crash
patterns.diagnosis: The cellular modem firmware crashed. Device may show 'No Service' or 'Searching'.

probabilities.description: Qualcomm/Intel modem IC soldering issue or failure.
Baseband power management IC not supplying correct voltage.
Carrier settings or iOS baseband firmware corruption.

repair_steps: 1. Check for 'No Service' or missing IMEI (Settings > General > About)
2. Reset network settings
3. DFU restore with latest iOS
4. If IMEI missing: baseband IC or BBPMU replacement (micro-soldering)
test_procedure: Check IMEI in Settings > General > About (if 'Unknown' = baseband dead)
Insert known-good SIM, check for cellular signal
Make a phone call
Check if airplane mode toggle works

patterns.title: NAND storage controller panic (ANS2)
patterns.diagnosis: The Apple NAND Storage v2 controller lost communication with flash storage. Critical storage issue.

probabilities.description: Flash storage IC failing. May need NAND replacement.
SoC-NAND communication line fault. Board-level issue.
Severe corruption. DFU restore may recover.

repair_steps: 1. DFU restore (may fail if NAND is physically damaged)
2. If restore fails: NAND chip replacement (expert micro-soldering)
3. If NAND replaced: reprogram with original serial numbers
4. Board swap if unrepairable
test_procedure: DFU restore attempt — does it complete?
After restore: write large file, verify data integrity
Check storage capacity: Settings > General > iPhone Storage
Monitor for boot loops

patterns.title: SMC panic assertion failure (iPhone 13+)
patterns.diagnosis: System Management Controller assertion failed. Common on iPhone 13+ when sensor arrays report faults.

probabilities.description: One or more sensor flex cables not seated. Check all connections.
Screen replacement missing sensor data. Use OEM or quality aftermarket with sensor transfer.
SMC IC or power rail fault. Advanced repair.

repair_steps: 1. Check all flex cable connections (display, battery, camera, charging)
2. If aftermarket screen: transfer OEM sensor flex or use compatible screen
3. Decode sensor array code in panic log to identify specific sensor
4. If all connections good: SMC issue, board-level repair
test_procedure: Check all sensors: proximity, ALS, microphones, barometer
Auto-brightness test
Monitor for panic recurrence after reseating all flexes

patterns.title: Sleep/Wake hang — audio codec or power issue
patterns.diagnosis: Device hung during sleep/wake transition. Often related to audio IC, amplifier, or power management.

probabilities.description: Audio codec not responding during sleep transition. iPhone 7 'audio IC disease'.
PMU not handling sleep states properly.
Backlight driver not responding during wake.

repair_steps: 1. DFU restore
2. Test audio: Voice Memos, speakerphone, headphones
3. If audio issues present: audio IC replacement (micro-soldering)
4. If no audio issues: check backlight IC and PMU
test_procedure: Lock/unlock phone 20 times rapidly
Lock phone, wait 5 min, unlock — any delay?
Voice Memos recording during sleep/wake cycles

patterns.title: Audio IC / codec failure
patterns.diagnosis: The Cirrus Logic audio codec IC is failing. Known as 'audio IC disease' on iPhone 7/7+.

probabilities.description: Cold solder joints under audio IC. Very common on iPhone 7. Reball required.
Pads lifted from board. Jumper wire repair needed.
IC itself failed. Full IC replacement.

repair_steps: 1. Test: does Voice Memos app open? (frozen = classic audio IC symptom)
2. Test speakerphone during call
3. Reball audio IC (remove, clean pads, reball, resolder)
4. If pads damaged: jumper wire repair to restore connections
test_procedure: Open Voice Memos — does it freeze/crash?
Speakerphone call — does speaker toggle work?
Record video — is there audio in playback?
Headphones: does audio output work via jack/lightning?

patterns.title: Tristar/Hydra USB charging IC failure
patterns.diagnosis: The Tristar (CBTL1612) or Hydra USB controller IC failed. Causes charging issues, 'This accessory may not be supported'.

probabilities.description: Tristar corroded from liquid exposure. IC replacement required.
IC degraded from using non-MFi cables. Replacement needed.
Lightning port dirty or damaged. Try flex replacement first.

repair_steps: 1. Clean lightning port (IPA + brush)
2. Try known-good MFi cable
3. Replace charging port flex cable
4. If persists: Tristar/Hydra IC replacement (micro-soldering)
test_procedure: Charge with MFi cable — does it charge?
Check for 'This accessory may not be supported' popup
Connect to iTunes/Finder — does computer recognize device?
Check charging current with USB ammeter

patterns.title: Face ID / TrueDepth camera system failure
patterns.diagnosis: The TrueDepth camera system (dot projector, flood illuminator, or IR camera) has failed.

probabilities.description: Dot projector laser module failed. Not individually replaceable on most models.
IR LED flood illuminator not functioning.
TrueDepth flex cable damaged during screen replacement.
VCSEL driver IC on board failed.

repair_steps: 1. Check if Face ID was working before last repair
2. Reseat front camera/TrueDepth flex cable
3. Inspect TrueDepth module under microscope
4. On iPhone 12+: TrueDepth module can be transferred to new screen
5. If module damaged: Face ID non-functional (no repair, device still usable)
test_procedure: Settings > Face ID & Passcode > Set Up Face ID
Front camera: does Portrait mode work?
Animoji: do face tracking animations work?
Check for 'Face ID is not available' error in Settings

patterns.title: NFC controller failure
patterns.diagnosis: The NFC controller IC (NXP) is not responding. Apple Pay and NFC will not work.

probabilities.description: NXP NFC controller IC failed. Micro-soldering replacement.
NFC antenna coil disconnected or damaged.
DFU restore may resolve.

repair_steps: 1. DFU restore
2. Check NFC antenna connections
3. Test Apple Pay enrollment
4. If persists: NFC IC replacement (micro-soldering)
test_procedure: Apple Pay: try adding a card
NFC tag: hold an NFC tag to back of phone
Express Transit: test with transit card if available

patterns.title: Ultra-Wideband (U1/U2) chip failure
patterns.diagnosis: The UWB chip is not responding. AirDrop direction finding and Precision Finding for AirTags won't work.

probabilities.description: U1/U2 chip failed. Not practically repairable.
UWB antenna cable not seated.
DFU restore may resolve.

repair_steps: 1. DFU restore
2. Check UWB antenna connections (near top of device)
3. If persists: UWB chip failure — device still functional without precision finding
test_procedure: AirDrop: test with nearby device — does direction arrow appear?
Find My: test Precision Finding with an AirTag
Nearby Interactions: test with another iPhone 11+

patterns.title: Camera ISP (Image Signal Processor) panic
patterns.diagnosis: The camera ISP crashed. Camera may show black screen or freeze the camera app.

probabilities.description: Camera flex cable damaged. Replace rear camera module.
Camera FPC not properly connected after repair.
ISP is part of SoC. DFU restore may help if software-related.

repair_steps: 1. Reseat rear camera flex cable
2. Try known-good camera module
3. DFU restore
4. If persists with good camera: ISP/SoC issue (board swap)
test_procedure: Open Camera app — does viewfinder show?
Take photo with each camera (wide, ultra-wide, telephoto)
Record video — does it complete without freezing?
Test flashlight toggle

patterns.title: Thermal shutdown — excessive temperature
patterns.diagnosis: Device shut down due to excessive temperature. May indicate cooling system or battery issue.

probabilities.description: Direct sunlight, hot car, charging while gaming. Not a defect.
Old or defective battery causing excess heat. Replace battery.
Component on board drawing excess current, generating heat.
Thermal interface material dried out (older devices).

repair_steps: 1. Check battery health (Settings > Battery > Battery Health)
2. If battery <80%: replace battery
3. Check for board-level shorts with thermal camera or DC power supply
4. If specific area hot: identify and replace faulty component
test_procedure: Use phone normally for 1h — does it get excessively hot?
Charge phone — is charging area extremely hot?
Use thermal camera to identify hot spots on board
Battery health percentage check

patterns.title: Power Management Unit (PMU) failure
patterns.diagnosis: The PMU/PMIC chip is not functioning correctly. Causes boot issues, random shutdowns, or no power.

probabilities.description: PMU chip (Dialog/Apple) failed. Micro-soldering replacement.
Short circuit on a power rail. Identify and fix shorted component.
Battery not providing correct voltage. Try new battery.

repair_steps: 1. Check boot current with DC power supply
2. Measure voltage rails with multimeter
3. Identify shorted rails with thermal camera
4. PMU replacement (advanced micro-soldering)
test_procedure: Does device boot?
Boot current pattern (normal is spike then settle)
Check all voltage rails with multimeter
Random shutdown frequency

patterns.title: Display backlight / brightness IC failure
patterns.diagnosis: The backlight driver IC failed. Screen may appear very dim or have no backlight.

probabilities.description: Backlight driver IC failed. Micro-soldering replacement.
Backlight filter on board blown (often from connecting screen while battery is connected).
Backlight lines in display flex damaged.

repair_steps: 1. Test: shine flashlight at screen — can you see image? (backlight dead but display works)
2. Check backlight filter/fuse on board
3. Replace backlight IC if filter is good
4. Try known-good display to rule out flex damage
test_procedure: Flashlight test: shine light through screen, look for faint image
Auto-brightness toggle test
Maximum brightness slider test
Connect to computer — does iTunes/Finder detect device?

patterns.title: I2C/SPI bus communication failure
patterns.diagnosis: Inter-chip communication bus failed. Multiple sensors/ICs may be affected.

probabilities.description: Corrosion on bus lines. Board cleaning and trace repair.
Drop damage causing broken traces.
A device on the I2C/SPI bus pulling lines low.

repair_steps: 1. Inspect board under microscope for corrosion/damage
2. Clean board with IPA and ultrasonic cleaner
3. Check I2C/SPI lines with oscilloscope
4. Repair broken traces or replace faulty ICs
test_procedure: Check which functions are not working (often multiple)
Test: touch, sensors, camera, WiFi systematically
Look for corrosion indicators (LCI stickers)
